memory.summary: My friends are heading to lunch, and since being with them matters most, I'll skip my assignment plan to join.
My friends are already ordering, so I'll rush over to join them before they finish.
They're so glad I showed up! Honestly, seeing them happy is way more important than that assignment anyway.
I let the moment pass, but my friends are still happy I was there. That assignment can wait; their happiness matters more right now.

My friends are so glad I showed up that the unread assignment doesn't even matter. Why stress about something that already passed when I'm having fun?

I just panicked and texted the group about missing the reading, but seeing them laugh it off makes me realize avoiding that awkwardness is worth it.

Avoid any awkwardness or confrontation regarding the missed reading deadline since I've already been forgiven and bringing it up again would ruin the mood.

goals.description: Launch into a wild, self-deprecating story about my past academic disasters to keep cohort_3 laughing and diffuse the tension immediately.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) makes the group's mood my only priority right now. I'm too avoidant to face the awkwardness of the missed deadline seriously, so I'll use impulsivity to distract everyone with chaos before anyone can get serious.

goals.description: Stare blankly at my laptop screen but refuse to open the assignment tab or think about the analysis section until tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: Acknowledging the unfinished work would break the spell of the evening and introduce stress I can't handle right now. My sleep discipline is low and risk tolerance is high, so I'll just assume the consequences will magically resolve themselves later. The immediate emotional safety of the group wins every time.
